headline: Survey at 16% completion; strong regional concentration risks representativeness.
collection_status: 220 of 1,350 responses collected (16% of target). Lindi dominates with 31% of responses; 8 regions active but uneven distribution. Pace requires acceleration to meet target by deadline.
key_findings: Teacher engagement strong: 107/220 (49%) are KiuFunza teachers; head teachers represent 22%, indicating solid core stakeholder participation.
Expansion priorities clear: 77 respondents prioritize expanding KiuFunza; 48 each want new programs and policy integration—scaling and institutionalization are top asks.
Scale readiness gap significant: Average readiness score 2.9/5 suggests systems/capacity concerns despite 4.4/5 partner satisfaction, indicating optimism-implementation mismatch.
Regional imbalance acute: Lindi (68), Tanga (43), Mara (40) account for 77% of responses; Singida (1), Mtwara (12), Songwe (13) severely underrepresented.
flags: Lindi overweighting risk: 31% concentration may skew findings; urgent need to mobilize collection in Singida, Mtwara, Songwe to ensure regional validity.
Implementation-readiness disconnect: High satisfaction (4.4) paired with low readiness (2.9) suggests stakeholders lack confidence in operational scaling despite partnership buy-in.
Research participation negligible: Only 3/220 mention research priority; evidence-based programming may be under-resourced in stakeholder perception.
recommendation: Immediately deploy targeted collection drives in underrepresented regions (target 200+ responses from Singida, Mtwara, Songwe, Shinyanga combined). Simultaneously, conduct rapid diagnostic on scale readiness barriers—interview 10-15 respondents scoring <3 to understand systems/resource gaps blocking implementation of expansion priorities.
